cpu_386_dx.name: Intel 386 DX
cpu_386_dx.description: 32-bit PC CPU. Opens the door to Mode 13h Frame Buffer tricks and raycasting voxel demos.

cpu_486_dx2_66.name: Intel 486 DX2-66
cpu_486_dx2_66.description: Doubles the internal clock. The engine behind first-generation texture-mapping demos.

cpu_pentium_100.name: Intel Pentium 100
cpu_pentium_100.description: Dual-pipeline superscalar. Chew through fixed-point 3D math without breaking a sweat.

cpu_pentium_ii_400.name: Intel Pentium II 400
cpu_pentium_ii_400.description: MMX/SSE; later paired with 3dfx Voodoo accelerators to drive 3D scenes.

cpu_athlon_700.name: AMD Athlon 700
cpu_athlon_700.description: AMD's first true enthusiast-grade contender. Loves shader-style procedural math.

cpu_pentium_iii_500.name: Intel Pentium III 500
cpu_pentium_iii_500.description: SSE intrinsics at last. Drives per-vertex transform/lighting demos.

gpu_voodoo_1.name: 3dfx Voodoo Graphics
gpu_voodoo_1.description: First consumer 3D accelerator with dedicated texture memory. Glide API era.

gpu_voodoo_2.name: 3dfx Voodoo 2 (SLI pair)
gpu_voodoo_2.description: Dual-board pass-through rendering. The knob for 800x600 megapixel terrain walks.

gpu_geforce_256.name: NVIDIA GeForce 256
gpu_geforce_256.description: Hardware transform & lighting (TnL). 32 MiB SDRAM. Era-defining release.

gpu_geforce2_gts.name: NVIDIA GeForce 2 GTS
gpu_geforce2_gts.description: A tweaked GeForce 256 with twice the fill rate. Sustained the late-3dfx era.

gpu_radeon_ddr.name: ATI Radeon DDR
gpu_radeon_ddr.description: Dual-channel DDR memory. Sturdier under 32-bit color rendering loads.

ram_8mb_72pin.name: 8 MiB 72-pin SIMM
ram_8mb_72pin.description: Required for 64KB intros and high-color Mode-X flat buffers.

ram_32mb_dimm.name: 32 MiB 168-pin DIMM
ram_32mb_dimm.description: Enables loading 64 MiB sample banks for GUS Ultrasound on the fly.

ram_128mb_dimm.name: 128 MiB SDRAM DIMM
ram_128mb_dimm.description: Required for 4K procedural intros using per-pixel iterators.

ram_512mb_ddr.name: 512 MiB DDR DIMM
ram_512mb_ddr.description: Heavy enough for raymarched distance fields and 64 MB texture caches.

hdd_540mb.name: 540 MiB IDE HDD
hdd_540mb.description: Spinning-platter storage. Hold dozens of MOD samples and multiple demo sources.

hdd_4gb.name: 4 GiB UDMA-33 HDD
hdd_4gb.description: Pioneered scripting languages' source caches and frame-accurate captures.

ssd_20gb.name: 20 GiB Solid-State Drive
ssd_20gb.description: Replaces spinning rust — no seek chirps during demo recordings, ever.

audio_sb16.name: SoundBlaster 16
audio_sb16.description: 16-bit stereo PCM at last. Multitrack modders' workhorse.

audio_gus.name: Gravis Ultrasound
audio_gus.description: Onboard wavetable memory — classic 32-voice MOD playback.

audio_gus_max.name: Gravis Ultrasound MAX
audio_gus_max.description: EMI-compatible, 1 MB sample RAM. Sample-heavy tracker modules shine here.

audio_awe32.name: SoundBlaster AWE32
audio_awe32.description: EMU8000 wavetable synth; brilliant for 64KB intros' in-house music engines.

monitor_14_crt.name: 14" CRT (VGA 640x480)
monitor_14_crt.description: Magnifying glass for your Mode 13h pixel arsenal.

monitor_17_crt.name: 17" Trinitron CRT
monitor_17_crt.description: Aperture grille shader-perfect, ideal for projecting 64KB demos to a party crowd.

monitor_19_lcd.name: 19" LCD Panel
monitor_19_lcd.description: Pixel-perfect preview; the era's "production monitor" of choice for 4K intros.
